questions.text: What is MCP (Model Context Protocol) in the context of Kiro?

options.label: A protocol that allows Kiro to connect to external tools and services
An internal mechanism that manages the conversation window context between model messages
A standard for synchronizing prompt history between different Kiro sessions
A package that bundles documentation and steering into a single installable module

questions.explanation: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a protocol that allows Kiro to connect to external tools and services, expanding its capabilities beyond the local development environment.

questions.text: MCP servers in Kiro can provide tools that are accessed through the Powers interface.
questions.explanation: When a Power includes MCP servers, their tools are accessed through Kiro's Powers interface, keeping the context focused while providing full access to capabilities on demand.

questions.text: Your team needs Kiro to access an internal documentation API. What is the best approach?

options.label: Create a custom MCP server that exposes the API as tools accessible by Kiro
Periodically copy the documentation to Steering files in the repository for Kiro to reference
Ask Kiro to access the API directly via HTTP
Install a generic documentation Power and expect it to access the internal API automatically

questions.explanation: The recommended approach is to create a custom MCP server that exposes the internal API as tools. This allows Kiro to access the documentation in a structured and secure way through the MCP protocol.
